annotations.label: Closed graph theorem (informal statement)

anchor.snippet: the theorem states that a linear operator between two Banach spaces is continuous if and only if the graph of the operator is closed

annotations.note: Mathlib's closed graph theorem proves the substantive direction: a linear map between Banach spaces with closed graph is continuous.

annotations.label: Bounded equals continuous for normed spaces

anchor.snippet: Since an operator between two normed spaces is a bounded linear operator if and only if it is a continuous linear operator

annotations.note: States that a map between normed spaces is linear and continuous iff it is a bounded linear map.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for closed graph

anchor.snippet: for the graph of T to be closed, it is enough that

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes exactly this sequential condition but folds it directly into a continuity conclusion (via IsSeqClosed.isClosed) rather than stating graph-closedness standalone.

annotations.label: Closedness in coarser topology implies closed in norm topology

anchor.snippet: if the graph of T is closed in some topology coarser than the norm topology, then it is closed in the norm topology

annotations.note: IsClosed.mono gives the general-topology fact that a set closed in a coarser topology is closed in any finer one.

annotations.label: Open mapping theorem deduced from closed graph theorem

anchor.snippet: the open mapping theorem can in turn be deduced from the closed graph theorem

annotations.note: The open mapping theorem is formalized, though Mathlib proves it directly via Baire rather than deducing it from the closed graph theorem.

annotations.label: Failure without completeness

anchor.snippet: one knows that the theorem fails without the completeness assumption

annotations.note: No counterexample showing failure of the closed graph theorem without completeness is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed graph theorem (Banach/Fréchet spaces)
annotations.note: Mathlib has the Banach-space case but not the more general Fréchet-space version stated in the theorem box.

annotations.label: Boundedness of Fourier transform via closed graph theorem

anchor.snippet: The Hausdorff–Young inequality says that the Fourier transformation

annotations.note: The Hausdorff–Young inequality / Lp-boundedness of the Fourier transform is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph inner product and graph norm

anchor.snippet: Define the graph inner product on

annotations.note: No graph inner product or graph norm structure on an operator's domain is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed operator iff graph is Banach
annotations.note: Mathlib defines a closed operator as having closed graph, but does not state the equivalence with the graph (under the graph norm) being a Banach space.

annotations.label: Bounded operator: closed iff domain closed
annotations.note: The LinearPMap closed-operator infrastructure exists, but the specific equivalence (for bounded operators, closed iff domain closed) is not stated.

annotations.label: Closed graph theorem (barrelled to Fréchet)
annotations.note: Barrelled spaces are defined (with Banach–Steinhaus), but no closed graph theorem from a barrelled space to a Fréchet space is proven.

annotations.label: Closed graph theorem between F-spaces
annotations.note: Mathlib has no closed graph theorem at the level of F-spaces (complete metrizable TVS); only the Banach case exists.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for continuity between F-spaces
annotations.note: The bundle of equivalent continuity/closed-graph conditions for maps between F-spaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pseudometrizable metrizable iff Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: A pseudometrizable space is metrizable if and only if it is Hausdorff

annotations.note: The T0/Hausdorff pseudometrizable → metrizable direction exists as an instance, and metrizable → T2 holds, but the biconditional is not packaged as one statement.

annotations.label: Closed map from ultrabarrelled into complete pseudometrizable TVS

anchor.value: Closed Graph Theorem [ 8 ] — Also, a clo

annotations.note: Ultrabarrelled spaces and this closed graph theorem variant are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed bounded map from infrabarreled into complete pseudometrizable LCS

anchor.value: Closed Graph Theorem — A closed and boun

annotations.note: Infrabarrelled spaces and this closed graph theorem variant are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed graph theorem (inductive limit of Baire TVSs, webbed codomain)
annotations.note: Webbed spaces and inductive-limit closed graph theorems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed surjective map onto ultrabarrelled space

anchor.value: Closed Graph Theorem [ 8 ] — A closed su

annotations.note: This ultrabarrelled-target closed/open mapping variant is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed graph theorem via open mapping property
annotations.note: This abstract closed-graph-via-open-mapping characterization at TVS generality is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polish space and Souslin space

anchor.snippet: a topological space is called a Polish space if it is a separable complete metrizable space

annotations.note: PolishSpace is defined exactly, but Souslin spaces (as a space-level class) are not; only analytic sets (MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet) exist.

annotations.label: K-sigma-delta space

anchor.snippet: if it is the countable intersection of countable unions of compact sets

annotations.note: No K-sigma-delta (Kσδ) space notion is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: K-analytic space

anchor.snippet: is called K-analytic if it is the continuous image of a

annotations.note: K-analytic spaces are not defined; MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet is a related but distinct (continuous image of Polish) notion.

annotations.label: Borel graph theorem

anchor.value: Borel Graph Theorem — Let [MATH] be line

annotations.note: The Borel graph theorem (continuity from Borel-measurable graph for Polish-type spaces) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized Borel graph theorem
annotations.note: The generalized (K-analytic / Souslin) Borel graph theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed operator into finite-dimensional TVS is continuous

anchor.snippet: closed linear operator from a Hausdorff locally convex TVS

annotations.note: Mathlib proves continuity of linear maps from a finite-dimensional domain, not continuity of a closed operator into a finite-dimensional codomain.

annotations.label: Closed linear operator

anchor.snippet: such an operator is called a closed linear operator

annotations.note: Mathlib defines a (possibly partially-defined) linear operator to be closed exactly when its graph is closed.
